tasks_completed.description: Updated _check_git_allowlist() to call match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() before the subagent firewall check
tasks_completed.changes: Added sentinel grant check at top of _check_git_allowlist() before the legacy path. Added match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command to import at line 92. task_id resolved via os.environ.get('CLAUDE_TASK_ID') or sid, matching userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh writer logic.
tasks_completed.rationale: pretool-git-privilege-guard.py ran as a parallel PreToolUse hook but never consulted the structured sentinel grant written by /allow. The sentinel honored by bash-safety.sh was invisible to the privilege guard, causing the guard to block commands the user had explicitly allowed. Fix mirrors the M2 design decision already implemented in pretool-bash-safety.sh:484.

tasks_completed.description: Created pytest test suite covering AC1-AC8
tasks_completed.changes: 8 tests covering: sentinel bypass for main-agent (AC1), sentinel bypass for subagent (AC2), no-grant default-deny (AC3), legacy grant main-agent (AC4), legacy subagent firewall preserved (AC5), sentinel no-overmatch (AC6), expired sentinel rejected (AC7), _evaluate_push() ordering preserved (AC8).
tasks_completed.rationale: All acceptance criteria are pytest-based per the BA spec. Tests verify both the new sentinel path and preservation of existing legacy behavior.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A - design gap not introduced by a single commit; _check_git_allowlist() was never updated when the sentinel grant system was added
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: _check_git_allowlist() only called read_grant_for_git_command() (legacy path). The structured sentinel grant system (task 20260519-211515) was added to pretool-bash-safety.sh but the parallel privilege guard hook was not updated to mirror the sentinel check.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Added match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() call in _check_git_allowlist() before the subagent firewall, mirroring the M2 decision already implemented in pretool-bash-safety.sh:484. The two parallel PreToolUse hooks now both honor sentinel grants consistently.

dev.qa_notes: Run pytest tests/generated/20260524-133650/ to verify all 8 ACs pass. Key scenarios to verify: (1) sentinel grant allows git ref-mutation commands for both main-agent and subagent contexts, (2) legacy grant path continues to work for main-agent, (3) subagent firewall preserved on legacy path only.

self_verification.notes: All 8 pytest tests pass (8 passed in 0.03s). Hook is a Python script with no compile step; py_compile implicitly verified by pytest import. Implementation matches BA spec 'After (proposed)' exactly at lines 144-162.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 4 findings: (1) BLOCKER: /allow git branch -D foo writer only emits op:git without args_contain — separate ticket per BA spec Won't Have (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh not in scope); (2) BLOCKER: sentinel-exists-but-no-match should suppress legacy matching — out of scope, BA spec only asked to add sentinel check before subagent firewall; (3) BLOCKER: compound Bash &&-chains — explicitly excluded per BA spec Won't Have ('&&-chained command multi-subcommand authorization — separate ticket'); (4) MAJOR: posttool-allowlist-consume.py does not consume subagent sentinels — out of scope, files_to_modify is pretool-git-privilege-guard.py only.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 codex findings classified as out_of_scope per BA spec explicit Won't Have list and scope boundary. The requirement ('sentinel grant honored by privilege guard for all agents') holds as implemented and verified by 8 passing tests. No implementation changes made in response to codex findings. Findings logged here for separate-ticket triage.

recommendations: Separate ticket: userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh writer should emit args_contain for multi-token /allow inputs (codex finding 1).
Separate ticket: sentinel-exists-but-no-match should suppress legacy matching in _check_git_allowlist() for defense-in-depth (codex finding 2).
Separate ticket: compound &&-chain authorization in match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() (codex finding 3).
Separate ticket: posttool-allowlist-consume.py should consume subagent sentinels consistently with the new pretool behavior (codex finding 4).
